name: Band Chest Fly
resistance_profile: Accommodating band resistance rises as the band stretches, so it is hardest at peak contraction when the hands meet and easiest at the stretched position.
cues: Step out to pre-tension the band before the first rep
Lead with the hands and squeeze at the finish
Keep the elbows fixed — don't press
execution_steps: Anchor a resistance band at chest height behind you, or run it across your upper back, holding an end in each hand.
Step forward into a staggered stance to remove slack, arms out to the sides with a slight elbow bend.
Brace the core and keep the elbows soft and fixed throughout.
Sweep the hands together in front of the chest in a wide arc, squeezing the pecs at the finish.
Control the band back out to the stretched position and repeat.
good_when: Training at home or travelling with minimal equipment
You want constant tension and a strong peak-contraction squeeze
You need shoulder-friendly chest isolation with accommodating resistance
bad_when: You want heavy absolute loading for the chest
You want maximal tension in the stretched position
No suitable anchor point is available
common_errors: Letting the band snap back uncontrolled
Bending the elbows into a press
Standing too close so there is no tension at the start
progressions: Use a thicker or shorter band for more tension
Add a pause at peak contraction
regressions: Anchor higher for a low-to-high fly with less resistance
